context_prose: tokenresolver is the API-token resolution component for the engram CLI. It is consumed by `cli` before any Anthropic LLM call (R7). At runtime it invokes its two true DI seams — `getenv` and `execCmd` (function values) — both of which ultimately drive S3 · Claude Code's host OS (env vars and the macOS Keychain via `security`). The `goos` string is a plain configuration value and not a DI seam, so it's omitted from the manifest under the new schema. The component encodes one strict architectural invariant: Resolve never returns a non-nil error. All failure modes collapse to `("", nil)` so callers can branch on empty-string alone.

edges.label: reads ENGRAM_API_TOKEN env / shells out to security find-generic-password

legend_items: Blue components = sibling components in c3-engram-cli-binary.md.
Grey = external systems (S3 · Claude Code carried over from L3 — host OS env + Keychain).
R-edges carry inline property IDs `[P…]` linking to the Property Ledger.
All edges traceable to a relationship in c3-engram-cli-binary.md.
name: tokenresolver

properties.name: Env precedence
properties.notes: Asserts the executor stays uncalled — no Keychain side-effect when env is set.
properties.statement: For all calls to Resolve, if `getenv("ENGRAM_API_TOKEN")` returns a non-empty string, Resolve returns that string and never invokes `execCmd`.

properties.name: Empty env not used
properties.notes: The `if token != ""` guard is what enables every keychain-branch test.
properties.statement: For all calls to Resolve, if `getenv("ENGRAM_API_TOKEN")` returns the empty string, Resolve does not return that empty string as the token; it falls through to the OS-gated Keychain branch.

properties.name: Non-darwin skips Keychain
properties.notes: Guards Linux/Windows callers from spurious `security` invocations.
properties.statement: For all calls to Resolve where env is empty and `goos != "darwin"`, Resolve returns `("", nil)` and never invokes `execCmd`.

properties.name: Keychain happy path
properties.notes: The single nested-field path documented in `keychainPayload`.
properties.statement: For all calls to Resolve on darwin where env is empty and `execCmd` returns valid JSON containing `claudeAiOauth.accessToken = T`, Resolve returns `(T, nil)`.

properties.name: Keychain exec error swallowed
properties.notes: `//nolint:nilerr` documents the intentional swallow. Keychain unavailable ≠ fatal.
properties.statement: For all calls to Resolve on darwin where `execCmd` returns a non-nil error, Resolve returns `("", nil)` — the error is not propagated.

properties.name: Malformed JSON swallowed
properties.notes: Second `//nolint:nilerr` site. Robustness against future Keychain output drift.
properties.statement: For all calls to Resolve on darwin where `execCmd` returns bytes that do not parse as JSON, Resolve returns `("", nil)`.

properties.name: Missing JSON field returns empty
properties.notes: Zero-value of the typed payload field is the empty string. Also covers explicit empty-string accessToken (test at line 46).
properties.statement: For all calls to Resolve on darwin where `execCmd` returns valid JSON without `claudeAiOauth.accessToken`, Resolve returns `("", nil)`.

properties.name: Resolve never errors
properties.notes: Architectural invariant: the doc comment guarantees Resolve's signature degrades to ("", nil) on every failure path. Every test asserts `err NotTo HaveOccurred`. Callers branch on empty-string alone.
properties.statement: For all inputs and all DI configurations, Resolve returns a nil error.

properties.name: No direct I/O
properties.notes: Architectural invariant aligned with the project's DI-everywhere rule. UNTESTED — there is no static-analysis check that the package's import set excludes `os`/`exec`/`runtime`.
properties.statement: For all code paths in package tokenresolver, no function calls `os.*`, `exec.*`, `runtime.*`, or any other I/O package directly; all OS interaction is mediated by injected `getenv`, `execCmd`, and `goos`.

properties.name: Keychain query parameters fixed
properties.notes: UNTESTED — existing tests ignore the args passed to execCmd. A drift in the service name would silently break Keychain lookup.
properties.statement: For all calls into resolveFromKeychain, `execCmd` is invoked with arguments `("security", "find-generic-password", "-s", "Claude Code-credentials", "-w")`.

properties.name: Context propagation
properties.notes: UNTESTED — no test cancels ctx and asserts execCmd received the cancelled context.
properties.statement: For all calls Resolve(ctx), the same ctx is forwarded into every `execCmd` invocation, so a cancelled context cancels the underlying `security` process.
